title: Garments Capital of the Philippines
description: Taytay is the primary source of ready-to-wear (RTW) clothing for much of the country. The Taytay Tiangge is a massive hub where local manufacturers sell high-quality garments at wholesale prices.

title: Woodcarving and Craftsmanship
description: While neighboring Angono is known for painting and Paete for woodcarving, Taytay has a deep history in high-quality furniture and woodwork, often referred to as the "Hambambalu" tradition.

title: Culinary Heritage
description: Taytay is known for its distinct version of Adobo sa Dilaw (using turmeric) and local snacks like Suman and Kalamay, and the all-famous Epalog.

title: Minor Basilica
description: The Minor Basilica and Parish of Saint John the Baptist serves as Taytay's spiritual and geographic anchor, a testament to the town's deep-rooted faith and centuries of rich colonial history, while perched on a hill overlooking the bustling commercial district
